name: Law Courts 8 Stair
description: Two 8 stairs one after another. Good run up with nice smooth floor. Good for getting gnarly. One of the most documented Newcastle spots in Uk touring videos. A proper classic!

name: Millenium Bridge
description: Slanted metal ledge hittable via one of the many benches

name: Baltic 4
description: Crusty 4 block. Jamie c has shut it down in the past.

name: Tyne Bridge Up Ledge
description: Very rounded up ledge nestled beneath the gateshead side of the tyne bridge

name: Tyne Bridge Ledge to Rail
description: About curb high to pop onto this rail has a just below shoulder height dismount accompanied by a heft drop on the other side

name: Quayside Ledge
description: Tall and crusty ledge. Fun one though.

name: Harry Bastard Banks
description: Legit bank spot. Not an easy one to skate but a fun session for sure and undercover so can be a saviour in the rain. Many classic tricks have gone down here over the years. Undercover drop down ledge just round the corner and a downhill curb in the carpark right there too. Plenty to be had near the banks!

name: Ledge Drop Ledge
description: This ledge drop ledge is fairly cursty and isn't made much better with the sandpaper like floor

name: Fat Buddah Manny
description: Modest height manny pad with a few hefty ledges

name: 55 Degrees up rail
description: Two identical long uprails, you gotta have your pop out game on lock though

name: Gap to Bench
description: One of the Toons more iconic spots Gap to Bench, gap the 2 to grind, slide, manny or send it over the full thing.

name: Church Ledge
description: Newly renovated Church ledge spot. Curved and straight ledges. Nice flatground. Pretty lit at night too.

name: China Town Ledge
description: Another toon classic. Small ledge and a taller one too. Just round the corner from the shop!

name: Monument 2/Manny Pad
description: situated in the heart of the city this spot can be skated as a two block or manny pad

name: Monument Benches
description: Monument has a selection of Marble dotted about but these two in a row are the go to (depending on the season could be surrounded by turf)

name: Blue Carpet 1/4
description: The not so blue carpet art installation turned skate spot. The blue tiled floor is pealed up and resting against the wall creating a short, whippy, pointed 1/4

name: Queens Banks
description: The classic spot! Given a new lease of life recently, this classic bank spot is back open to shred. Two crusty banks with good run-up down the ramp and a brick wall at the top for spicing it up a bit!

name: Smackhead Double
description: Maybe the gnarliest spot in Toon. A fat 3 flat 3 set. Can skate the two 3's separately or be like Scott Cherry and a select few others and send the full thing. If you haven't seen Scott's classic Sw heel get it watched. Great run up, quite a sketchy location however.

name: Cycle Path/Bank
description: One of the newer town spots. Two separate bits along John Dobson Street. One undercover, one in the open. Lovely smooth bike path with slappy curbs for days and good for manuals. The undercover section also has a skateable ledge and crusty pop on banks that have been getting hammers sent on them in the Toon for many years! Really fun warm up spot!

name: Library Ledge
description: A not so skated spot these days... 2 modest hieght ledges begging for some grind to grind action. A small block at the end offers up a gap to grind oppertunity for the quicker footed out there.

name: Library Manny Pad & Ledge
description: Long thing manny pad and grind/slide ledge at the end, ideally skated after hours. Good spot though

name: Civic Benches
description: A line of marble benches, a little on the high side with a tiled floor that leaves a lot to be desired

name: Exi Park
description: The only city centre skatepark. Over 20 years old and pretty crust. Classic spot though, amazing bowl.

name: Smooth Path
description: Exactly what the name suggests, a long smooth path for warming up or endless games of skate

name: Haymarket
description: Once the weekend meeting spot for the scene now a very rarely touched manny pad and stair set

name: Uni Stairs/Slappy Ledge
description: A 3 flat 3 flat 5 with a hard to skate out ledge and a few ride on slappie ledges

name: The Core
description: Maybe the best spot Newcastle has to offer. Everything you could want you'll find at the core. Ledges galore, manny pads, gaps. It has the lot. Sometimes a kickout but security also fairly lenient at times. If you're skating Newcastle you have to check it out!

name: College Block 3
description: Super Crusty 3 block

name: College Rail
description: Wooden kinked Down Rail

name: Bike Garage Ledge To Bank
description: One push run up ledge drop into a long mellow bank, very much not skateable during working hours

name: 55 Degrees North Underground Rails
description: If you can find your way through the security gate theres 2 handrails and an out rail to be skated, plenty of NBD's to be had

name: Castle Leazes Bank
description: a short steep bank situated in the heart of Castle Leazes syudent accomodation

name: Castle Leazes Rail
description: Pretty perfect downrail. Bs if you're regs!

name: Leazes 13
description: Massive 13 only sessioned by a mere few og's! Crust these days but still sendable

name: Leazes Gap
description: A smooth tarmac run up to gap over and down a selection of rocks & shrubbery

name: Leazes Pond Rail
description: Not the greatest of up rails with a lot of board in water potentional

name: 5 Bridges
description: 5 Bridges has been skated as far back as the early 80's (Possibly sooner) and is still going strong today. It started life as arguably the best street spot in the North and with the addition of a few blocks, rails and a kicker soon became an iconic home to northern skating. The uneven floor and rounded ledges give it a certain character and charm that can't be matched. Love it or hate it it's the best your going to get around here

name: Clock Slappy Ledge
description: Buttery smooth floor with slappy ledge/manny pad

name: Tesco Downhill Manny
description: Curb high downhill manny pad, heavy traffic warning on this one

name: Tesco Ledge
description: Proper height ledge only a few have skated, Cougars Bs Noseblunt being one of the highlights

name: Tesco Double
description: 3 flat 7 the site of a monster kickflip from Adam Stoddart

name: Boathouse Ledge
description: Crusty ledge over a gap. Rough floor but sick spot.

name: Toffee Factory Ledge
description: Really small outledge. Super chill spot. Weekend or after hours recommended.

name: Cluny 4
description: Gnarly 4 block. Ledges to be skated too. Nice spot for a pint also!

name: White Blocks
description: A spot for the quick footed manny technicians out there

name: River 5
description: Another Toon classic! Pretty perfect 5 stair with great run up and smooth ground! Just up the river from the Tyne Bridge. Great for summer sessions.

name: Disco Ledge
description: Great ledge. Really smooth floor. Flatbars too, mainly a weekend session as it's a carpark that's full during the week.

name: Carpark Bank
description: A rough floored bank which has seen many a trick sent into over the years and a mosher drop for the not so faint of heart

name: Red Brick 1/4's
description: A steep whippy red brick 1/4 situated in a small housing estate. You might get the boot depending on how the locals are feeling

name: Teletubbies bank
description: a mellow astro turf bank for doing tricks into & manny pad

name: Newcastle College
description: Plenty to offer at the college but high kickout factor

name: St James Undercover
description: Multi story carpark so under cover. Smooth flat and crusty curb. Floor 5 if you know, you know! Cluny

name: China Town Wallrides
description: Tall and super crusty wallrides

name: White Ledge
description: 'Best Ledge in town' - Jackie Young

name: Arena
description: A collection of stairs ranging from a 2 to a 5 with a slappy curb and some ledges thrown in there.

name: Barker & Stonehouse Ledge
description: Selection of Marble window ledges, best to be skated after working hours

name: Asda Bank
description: brink bank topped by a fence screaming for an ollie over

name: Civic 8
description: Ground has seen better days but this remains one of the more legit stair sets the toon has to offer

name: Jesmond Tunnel
description: Undercover banked manny pads/wallride

name: Buffalo Joes Electrical Box
description: Absolute hammer spot, only ever touched by Connor North... Bs 5050

name: Law Courts Leccy Box
description: Bump to grind

name: Uni Planter
description: thin red brick run up to metal trimmed planter

name: Uni Red Brick Hubba
description: 1 push run up to red brick hubba

name: Uni Wall Gap
description: Thin run up & landing

name: Uni Bin Bonk
description: Quick footed drop in to bin bonker

name: Autosounds Rail
description: Tarmac bump to grind

name: Carpark Wallride
description: Soft wheels recommended for the now terrible ground

name: Girder Gap
description: reasonably high, slightly slanted girder grind over a grate

name: Warwick Street Curbs
description: Just round the corner from the white ledge, a good seletion of curbs have been rubbricked and waxed. Plenty fun to be had here

name: 'Bump' To Flatgap
description: Very mellow bump to flatgap, fun spot especially as there's a ledge to the side that can be used for grind/slide to manny tricks

name: Bollard gap
description: Thin wall run-up to a gap over a bollard! Ghetto sean ollied back in the day but everything else NBD

name: Connor North Fence Gap
description: Gnarly flatgap over a fence onto the road. Only stepped to by Connor North for his ender in his Gasoline section

name: Shop Front Wall Gap
description: Good gap over a wall but right on a shop front open all day, everyday. First try spot this one

name: Medieval Bank
description: Crusty cobble bank with slappy in potential. Curb at the bottom so only really for going in. Nothing done other than a Jackie Young Kickflip

name: Jesmond Metro drop ledges
description: Good spot recently unlocked by an OG Bmx'r. Drop down ledges on a hill with smooth floor hittable both ways for each stance.

name: Manny pad spot
description: One of the better Manny spots in Town with two manny pads one after another on smooth floor. Weekend or after hours spot
Hidden and rarely skated, lot's of stairs to be had. Pretty perfect until the 9 when the landing is pretty rough. Plenty to be had.

name: Coast Road Roundabout ledge
description: Pretty crusty ledge but good floor and can skate it all day everyday

name: The Tunnel
description: Only recently skated, this is maybe the best undercover spot we have to offer now Bridges is gone (Rip) Smooth floor and ledge either side. (Usually) stays dry

name: Cobble Gap Ledge
description: Ledge over cobbles. Only really skateable one way (BS Regular, FS Goofy)

name: Herons Ride On Grinds
description: Ride on grinds out the back of Herons. Probably a bust when the shops open

name: Heaton Estate Manny Pads
description: Decent manny spot. Probably get some riff raff interactions here.

name: Byker Baths Ollie Up Then 6
description: Ollie up a curb then quick feet 6 stair. Good spot. Manny potential as well as stair tricks.

name: Byker Metro hubba
description: Mellow downledge. FS for Goofy, BS for regs. Probs best with a crew here.

name: Bank to wallride Tunnel
description: Cobble banks to wallride. Good spot but hard to skate. Shoutout Dan Main on the bs wallride

name: Blue Rails
description: Lot's of flatbar potential right outside the Wastey. Floor not amazing but good rails

name: Quayside 4 then 3
description: Another decent stair spot. Best to have a spotter though as board in the river potential

name: Quayside 6
description: Pretty big 6 stair. Good runup though. Again best to have a spotter

name: Pilgrim street ledge to double bank
description: Slappy height ledge into double bank. Gnarly spot. Not even built when this map image was taken. Props to Matty Smith for SW K grind and in.

name: Red Marble wall ledge
description: Weird ledge spot against a wall. Nice red marble. Not an easy one to skate as a weird round shape

name: Hidden 4
description: 4 stair. No kickout bur floor has seen better days

name: Civic Manny pad and gap
description: Pretty perfect manny pad to drop. Also a big gap skating it the other way

name: Wallie Ledge
description: Wallie to grind/slide ledge. Ledge has seen better days could do with some fixing up.

name: Heaton Park Mini Rails
description: Mini flatbars in a nice park. Good for a mess about. Couple kinkers to spice it up. Nice for chilling especially in summer

name: Bench Hillbomb
description: Metal bench on a hillbomb. Trick in at the top, powerslide, ledge trick, bomb the hill. FS for Regs

name: Curved Boardslide ledge
description: Legit spot for slides. Floor has seen better days but still skateable

name: Mega Hill Bomb
description: Just a big hill. Recently tarmaced so perfectly smooth. Best with a spotter as a bus route and blind corner at the bottom

name: Hilton Hotel 6's
description: Two pretty good 6 stairs in a row. Could be a bust

name: Tyne Bridge Flat Bars
description: Flat bars on a mellow hill. BS for regs

name: Tyne Bridge Big Set
description: Hefty stair set (maybe a 10) with slightly uphill landing. Gate which needs to be open to hit it

name: UNion Rooms
description: Pretty legit in and out spot. Busy location. Prudhoe Burger with the ollie in tre out

name: Central Statue Manny and Wallie
description: Stone manny pad and pretty hefty wallie out into the road

name: Pilgrim Drop down manny and grind
description: Brand new. Drop down manny pad ledges. Recently knobusted, so can grind through the drops. Really good spot

name: Taco Bell ledge to manny
description: Marble Ledge with potential to go to manny and off the curb

name: Core Flat Ledge
description: Recently bondo's to be made skateable. Pretty perfect ledge and good floor. Quite tall but nothing crazy

name: One Block, Two Block, Three Block
description: Decent gaps and can work your way up size wise. Not amazing run-up but skateable

name: Core Down Ledges
description: Pretty sick donwledges. One available for mega distance the other with a cap in the middle. FS and BS board done all the way already

name: Boardslide ledge and Core gap
description: Pretty perfect gap and ledge for boardslides and grinds

name: Downhill ledge and Ollie up to bin
description: Ledges on a downhill with the last one having to the added option of ollie up and onto / over a bin after

name: Wooden Flat ledges and in and out pad
description: 4 great wooden flat ledges and an interesting in and out spot. Plenty fun to be had here

name: Core Manny Pad
description: Circlular pad that probably looks better than it is, but worth a sesh if you're at the core. Good curbs just up the road

name: Undercover ledges
description: One of towns better covered spots. Good floor with two wooden ledges. Can get wet if sideways rain

name: Bump to bench
description: Gnarly drain to bench gap. Only scotch has stepped to this one

name: The Scorpions Tail (Floating 1/4)
description: One of newcastle's gnarliest spots. Floating 1/4 about a story up. Only Jamie Allen has hit it

name: RVI 5
description: Tiny 5 stair. Good fun. Kickout though

name: RVI Mini Wallenburg
description: Looks exactly like a micro wallenburg. Kickout though.

name: Cobble gap
description: Massive cobble flat gap. Jamie C HOWMAN

name: Metal Planters
description: Planters with silver coping for grinds and slides. Quite tall and quick bust

name: Ollie up and out bench
description: Fast feet Ollie up to tricks over and out

name: Autosounds 7
description: Skinny but small 7 stair. Curb at the top so manny potential

name: Times square curved flatbar
description: Rarely touched curved flatbar. Pretty legit. BS for regs

name: Royal mail gap
description: Fun little gap at the back of a royal mail depot

name: Two Block
description: Decent twoblock with a curb high block to clear at the top. Quick kickout

name: Carpark ollie over and off
description: Over a fence and fast feet gap to road

name: Marble Block
description: Pretty good marble ledge which has recently had the stoppers off. 5 stair after for lines but quick kick on the stairs

name: Civic outledge / manny pad
description: Marble outledge and manny pad. Floor is rough and the ledge is battered but fun for manny's

name: Uni mellow banks
description: Super mellow marble banks and a wallride. Fun ride but quick kickout

name: Freeman Multi story manny pad
description: Manny pad to drop on the top of the multi story. Sick spot. Decent chance of kickout though

name: Long downhill bank
description: Downhill bank for tricks in at the top

name: 3 up, 3 down
description: 3 stair up, 3 down. Big pop up required but good spot

name: Quorum Curved ledge
description: Not much space around it but grinds super good

name: Quorum Slappy bank
description: Really fun spot. Mellow bank for tricks in with slappy potential at the top

name: Shieldfield estate outledges
description: Good outledges and crusty 6 stair

name: Taxman Gap
description: Brand New. Freshest spot in town. Really fun little gap, wooden ledges to grind and slide. No kickout, for now

name: Tindy ledge and Boardslide ledge
description: Super smooth flat. Tiny ledge and ledge for boardslides, pop out only though.
